title: Work & School
shortSummary: Talk about your job, studies, and daily working life in German.
culturalNote: German working culture values clear structure, directness, and a strong separation between work and private life. Meetings start on time, plans are made in advance, and the famous dual system (duale Ausbildung) combines vocational training with on-the-job apprenticeships. Job titles and qualifications carry real weight, so people often state their profession with quiet pride.

keyPhrases.english: What do you do for work?

keyPhrases.german: Ich arbeite von neun bis fünf
keyPhrases.english: I work from nine to five
keyPhrases.pronunciation: ikh AR-by-tuh fon noyn bis foonf

keyPhrases.english: I have the day off today

grammar.explanation: Use 'als' to say in what role you work and 'bei' to name the employer: 'Ich arbeite als Ingenieurin bei Siemens' (I work as an engineer at Siemens). Both are common in introductions.
grammar.commonMistake: Mixing them: use 'als' for the role and 'bei' for the company, not the reverse.

grammar.explanation: When a sentence starts with a time phrase, the verb stays in second position and the subject moves after it: 'Am Montag habe ich ein Meeting'.

examples.de: Heute arbeite ich von zu Hause.

grammar.commonMistake: Keeping subject-verb order after a time phrase: it is 'Heute arbeite ich', not 'Heute ich arbeite'.

dialogue.setup: At a party, Nora and David get to know each other and talk about work.

lines.speaker: Nora
lines.german: Und was machst du beruflich, David?
lines.english: And what do you do for work, David?

lines.speaker: David
lines.german: Ich arbeite als Programmierer bei einer kleinen Firma.
lines.english: I work as a programmer at a small company.

lines.speaker: Nora
lines.english: Interesting! Do you like the work?

lines.speaker: David
lines.german: Ja, sehr. Und du? Was machst du?
lines.english: Yes, very much. And you? What do you do?

lines.speaker: Nora
lines.german: Ich studiere noch. Ich studiere Medizin an der Universität.
lines.english: I'm still studying. I study medicine at university.

lines.speaker: Nora
lines.german: Ja, aber es macht mir Spaß. Schönen Feierabend noch!
lines.english: Yes, but I enjoy it. Have a nice evening!

quiz.question: How do you ask 'What do you do for work?' (informal)
Ich arbeite _____ Lehrer. (in the role of)
quiz.explanation: als introduces the role: als Lehrer (as a teacher).

quiz.question: Which word means 'colleague' (female)?
quiz.explanation: die Kollegin is a female colleague; der Kollege is male.

quiz.question: What does 'Feierabend' mean?
quiz.explanation: Feierabend is the cherished moment when the workday ends.

quiz.question: Arrange: bei / ich / Siemens / arbeite
quiz.explanation: bei names the employer; verb arbeite stays in second position.
